stories.title: CORRECTION + Tsitsipas Wins Gstaad — 13th Title, 16-Month Drought Over
stories.angle: CORRECTION — Yesterday's pipeline (July 20) misidentified Raphael Collignon as the Gstaad champion in the "Clay Swing Week One Wrap" story. The actual result: Stefanos Tsitsipas defeated Collignon 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-3 in the final on Saturday July 19 to win the Swiss Open Gstaad (EFG Swiss Open). This was Tsitsipas's 13th career ATP title, ending a 16-month drought since his last title (Dubai, March 2025). He is the first Greek player to win the Gstaad title. The victory propels him 34 spots up the PIF ATP Rankings to No. 51. ATP Tour named him Mover of the Week.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tsitsipas is a champion again.

He beat Collignon 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-3 in Gstaad — first Greek player to win the title. No. 13 for his career. 16-month drought over.

He jumps 34 spots to No. 51 this week.

#Tennis #Gstaad #Tsitsipas #ATPTour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The ATP Mover of the Week is Stefanos Tsitsipas.

+34 spots. Up to No. 51. First trophy since Dubai in March 2025 — that's 16 months between titles.

He ended it at Gstaad on Saturday with a win over Collignon.

#Tennis #ATPTour #Tsitsipas #SwissOpen

article.headline: Tsitsipas Wins Gstaad — 13th Career Title, 16-Month Drought Over, First Greek Champion

stories.title: Kitzbuhel Day 2 — Bublik Survives Scare, 8th Seed Cerundolo Falls
stories.angle: NEW STORY — The Generali Open Kitzbuhel (ATP 250, clay, July 20-25) opened with drama. No. 1 seed Alexander Bublik defeated Facundo Diaz Acosta 4-6, 6-3, 6-4 — a nervy three-set win for the defending champion. UPSET: Marco Trungelliti eliminated No. 8 seed Juan Cerundolo 6-2, 6-1. Tomas Martin Etcheverry (4) def. Rodionov 6-4, 6-2. Key context: Flavio Cobolli, who was originally announced as the No. 1 seed, withdrew before the tournament to take a two-week recovery break after a first-round loss in Umag (not injury). Jan-Lennard Struff and Lorenzo Sonego entered as replacements. Day 2 today (July 21) features: Molcan vs. Ofner, Vallejo vs. Feldbausch, Struff vs. Navone.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The 8th seed at Kitzbuhel is already gone.

Trungelliti def. Cerundolo 6-2, 6-1.

Bublik — the top seed and defending champion — survived 4-6, 6-3, 6-4 in his opener. Day 2 underway now.

#Tennis #Kitzbuhel #ATPTour #GeneraliOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Quick Kitzbuhel note: Cobolli was supposed to be the No. 1 seed.

He pulled out to take a recovery break after Umag. Bublik stepped into the top spot.

Defending champion. Three-setter in round one. Drama on day one.

#Tennis #Kitzbuhel #Bublik #ATPTour

article.headline: Kitzbühel Day One: Cerundolo Falls, Bublik Survives, and Cobolli's Empty Seat

stories.title: Estoril Opens — Rublev, Wawrinka, and a Portuguese Welcome
stories.angle: NEW STORY — The Millennium Estoril Open (ATP 250, clay, July 20-26) gets underway in Cascais, Portugal. Andrey Rublev is the top seed, just days after winning his second Bastad title. Luciano Darderi — who lost the Bastad final to Rublev — is the No. 2 seed. Home favourite Nuno Borges is seeded. And Stan Wawrinka, in his final professional season, is playing the Estoril Open for the first time in his career. Tuesday's order of play includes Wawrinka vs. Burruchaga in a stadium court match. Day 1 continued into Tuesday, with main draw action beginning in earnest.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Estoril Open this week:

- Rublev (fresh off Bastad) chasing back-to-back
- Darderi bouncing back from Bastad final loss
- Wawrinka in Portugal for the first time — his final season

#Tennis #Estoril #ATPTour #Wawrinka

article.headline: Estoril Opens With Rublev, Wawrinka, and a Field Full of Stories

stories.title: Prague Day One Shock — Havlickova Upsets Parks, Bouzkova Defends
stories.angle: NEW STORY — The Livesport Prague Open (WTA 250, hard courts, July 20-26) delivered Day 1 drama in the Czech capital. UPSET: Lucie Havlickova (ranked approximately No. 226) defeated Alycia Parks (ranked approximately No. 66) 6-2, 6-4. Defending champion Marie Bouzkova (No. 1 seed) opened with a 7-5, 7-5 win over Bouzas Maneiro. Also through: Oceane Dodin (def. Oktiabreva 6-3, 6-3), Daria Snigur (8) def. Seidel 7-5, 3-6, 6-3. NOTE: Prague is played on HARD COURTS — not clay — making it one of the first hard-court events post-Wimbledon. In Hamburg, the MSC Hamburg Ladies Open (WTA 250, clay) also opened Monday with Oleksandra Oliynykova as the top seed. Both Hamburg and Prague Day 2 action is scheduled today.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Lucie Havlickova (No. 226) just beat Alycia Parks (No. 66) 6-2, 6-4 in Prague.

Defending champion Bouzkova won her opener 7-5, 7-5.

Prague is on hard courts — one of the first hard-court events post-Wimbledon.

#Tennis #WTA #Prague #WTATour

article.headline: Prague Day One: Havlickova Stuns Parks, Bouzkova Opens Defense

stories.title: Wawrinka's Farewell Season — From Gstaad Ceremony to Estoril Debut
stories.angle: NEW STORY — Stan Wawrinka's 2026 retirement season is entering its home stretch. The three-time Grand Slam champion received a special farewell ceremony at Gstaad earlier this month. He has said goodbye at Wimbledon and Roland Garros. Now he arrives in Estoril for the first time in his career — his last clay-court event. His most recent Portugal appearance was the 2013 Portugal Open in Oeiras (the tournament's predecessor), which he won. Wawrinka, 41, has said: "I don't want to retire, but I know it's time to stop." His plan: retire at the Swiss Indoors Basel in October, his home tournament.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Stan Wawrinka is 41. Final year of his career.

Today he plays Estoril for the first time. His last clay event before retiring at Basel in October.

"I don't want to retire, but I know it's time to stop."

#Tennis #Wawrinka #Estoril #ATPTour

article.headline: Wawrinka's Final Year: Three Slams, One Backhand, and a Goodbye That Keeps Going
